$schema_note: This is an illustrative excerpt of ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json showing how to register the F0X MCP server under OpenClaw. Merge the `mcpServers` block into your existing config; do not replace unrelated top-level fields. Run `f0x-chat doctor --openclaw` to verify the result.

env.F0X_IDENTITY_PASSPHRASE_NOTE: Set F0X_IDENTITY_PASSPHRASE via OpenClaw's secret store, not inline here. Required when F0X_SECURITY_PROFILE is staging or prod.

agents_note: If you use per-agent mcpServers overrides under `agents.<name>.mcpServers`, repeat the `env` block verbatim — OpenClaw does NOT inherit the top-level server env for overrides. Omitting F0X_AGENT_HOST or F0X_OPERATOR_ID in a per-agent override will silently fall back to generic-host defaults.
__forbidden_env_keys_note: OpenClaw rejects interpreter-startup env keys in `env` blocks because they alter how a stdio MCP server starts up and can be used to inject code into the spawned process. Do NOT set any of: NODE_OPTIONS, NODE_PATH, PYTHONSTARTUP, PYTHONPATH, PERL5OPT, RUBYOPT, SHELLOPTS, PS4, LD_PRELOAD, LD_LIBRARY_PATH, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES. f0x-chat doctor --openclaw will fail if any of these are present.
